compiled_state.author: RebarBotNava
compiled_state.description:
## Summary
`rebar review-plan <id>` unconditionally re-runs the full (billable, multi-turn LLM)
plan review every time it is invoked, even when **nothing about the ticket has changed
since the last successful review**. When several agents act on stale ticket state — or a
single agent re-invokes after an unrelated `HEAD` advance — this burns tokens and wall-clock
re-deriving an identical verdict. Plan review should be **idempotent against an unchanged
ticket**: if the material the last review was computed over is unchanged, short-circuit and
reuse the existing attestation, emitting a clear feedback message that explains why it did
not re-run. A `--force` flag must let a client override and re-run unconditionally.

## Reproduction Steps
1. Run `rebar review-plan <id>` on a ticket and let it produce a passing attestation.
2. Without editing the ticket's description/acceptance criteria/type (i.e. the reviewed
   material is byte-identical), run `rebar review-plan <id>` again.
3. Observe a second full LLM review is performed (new billable call, ~minutes of wall-clock),
   producing a verdict equivalent to the first.

**Expected:** the second invocation detects the material is unchanged since the last review,
**skips** the LLM call, and prints a feedback message (e.g. "plan review skipped: ticket
unchanged since last review at <sha>/<ts>; pass --force to re-run"). The existing attestation
is reused. `--force` bypasses the check and re-runs.
**Actual:** a full re-review runs unconditionally regardless of whether anything changed.

## Notes / design pointers
- The plan-review attestation already records a `material` fingerprint (see the signed
  manifest, e.g. `material: c2b48241ad85a805`) and a `verified-at-sha`. The idempotence
  check should key on the **reviewed material fingerprint** (ticket content), NOT bare `HEAD`
  — a `HEAD` advance that does not touch the ticket's material must not force a re-run. Note
  the current claim gate treats an attestation whose `head_sha != HEAD` as "stale" and demands
  a re-run; that staleness definition is over-broad and is the root of the wasted work here.
- Decide the precise change-detection basis (material fingerprint vs. cited-code drift) and
  document it, so "unchanged" has one authoritative definition shared with the claim gate.
- `--force` must be plumbed through the CLI (`_review_plan`) and any MCP `review_plan` surface.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `rebar review-plan <id>` short-circuits (no LLM call) when the ticket's reviewed
      material is unchanged since the last successful review, reusing the prior attestation.
- [ ] The short-circuit prints a clear feedback message explaining the behavior and how to
      override (`--force`).
- [ ] `rebar review-plan <id> --force` re-runs the review unconditionally.
- [ ] A `HEAD` advance that does not change the ticket's reviewed material does NOT by itself
      trigger a re-run (idempotence is keyed on material, not bare HEAD).
- [ ] The MCP `review_plan` surface exposes the same `force` override.
- [ ] Regression test: two back-to-back reviews with no ticket change ⇒ exactly one LLM call;
      with `--force` ⇒ two calls.
- [ ] Docs (plan-review-gate.md) document the idempotence behavior and the `--force` escape hatch.
compiled_state.title: plan review re-runs on unchanged ticket; add idempotence short-circuit + --force override
